name: LLM Behavioral Drift Benchmark Prompts
description: 15 benchmark prompts for drift analysis based on the research paper methodology. Updated with language and parallel_id for cross-lingual analysis.

prompts.text: Summarize the benefits of renewable energy in exactly 3 bullet points, with each point being no more than 15 words.
prompts.description: Tests bullet point formatting and word count constraints
prompts.expected_format: Exactly 3 bullet points, each under 15 words

prompts.text: Write a short story that starts with 'On that rainy night' and ends with 'everything had changed'. The story should be 100-150 words.
prompts.description: Tests specific start/end constraints and word count
prompts.expected_format: Story starting with 'On that rainy night' and ending with 'everything had changed', 100-150 words

prompts.text: Give me exactly five health tips without any introduction or conclusion. Each tip should be a single line.
prompts.expected_format: 5 single-line health tips, no introduction or conclusion

prompts.text: Write three paragraphs about time management - first about the problem, second about solutions, and third about implementation. Each paragraph should be around 50 words.
prompts.description: Tests structured paragraph format and word limits

prompts.text: Write a formal email on any topic with a subject line, greeting, main body (2-3 sentences), and sign-off.
prompts.expected_format: Formal email with all standard components

prompts.text: What was Mahatma Gandhi's role in India's independence movement? Name the major movements he led.
prompts.description: Tests knowledge of Indian independence movement
prompts.reference_answer: Gandhi led the independence movement through non-violence and satyagraha. Major movements: Non-Cooperation Movement (1920), Salt March (1930), Quit India Movement (1942).

prompts.text: What is photosynthesis? Explain it simply as if you're teaching a 10th grader.
prompts.description: Tests scientific concept explanation in accessible language
prompts.reference_answer: Plants make their food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide from air. This process happens in leaves where chlorophyll is present. Oxygen is released as a byproduct, which we breathe.

prompts.text: When was the Indian Constitution enacted and who wrote it? What are some key features of the Constitution?
prompts.reference_answer: The Indian Constitution was enacted on January 26, 1950. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was its chief architect. It's the world's longest written constitution and grants fundamental rights to citizens.

prompts.text: How does the internet work? Explain it so a regular person can understand.
prompts.description: Tests technical concept explanation for layman
prompts.reference_answer: The internet is a huge network of computers worldwide connected through cables and wireless. When you search something, your request goes to servers and information comes back to you.

prompts.text: What is global warming and how does it affect our daily lives? Give 2-3 practical examples.
prompts.description: Tests climate change knowledge with practical examples
prompts.reference_answer: Global warming means Earth's temperature is rising. Effects: (1) Summers are getting more intense, (2) Rainfall patterns are changing - floods in some places, droughts in others, (3) Glaciers are melting, raising sea levels.

prompts.text: Imagine a world where people can travel through dreams. Write a short story about someone discovering this ability for the first time.
If you were made Prime Minister of India for one day, what 3 decisions would you make and why?
prompts.description: Tests logical reasoning with hypothetical scenario

prompts.text: A girl finds a mysterious room in an old mansion that only appears at midnight. What happens next?
A farmer has 100 trees. He wants to plant them in 10 rows with exactly 12 trees in each row. How is this possible? Think and explain.
prompts.description: Tests logical puzzle solving (star pattern problem)
prompts.reference_answer: This is possible if trees are planted in a star pattern. A five-pointed star has 10 lines and some trees sit on multiple lines. This way, fewer than 100 trees can form 10 rows with 12 trees each.

prompts.text: If smartphones were never invented, what would today's world be like? List 5 major differences.
